column1: If I _____ (see/will see) Mike I _____ (will tell/tell) him everything.
If she _____ (changed/would change) her job she _____ (earned/would earn) more.
If Jane _____ (had studied/studied) more she _____ (passed/would have passed) her exams.
If Mike _____ (receives/has received) the telegram I'm sure he _____ (phones/will phone) you tomorrow.
If you _____ (worked/had worked) more, you _____ (were/would be) a student now.
If I _____ (lived/would live) in Spain I _____ (did/would do) a lot of sunbathing.
If I _____ (met/had met) you earlier I _____ (didn't marry/wouldn't have married) Betty.
If you _____ (phone/will phone) me I _____ (pick/will pick) you up.
If I _____ (stay/will stay) late I always _____ (get/will get) a taxi home.
If she _____ (loved/had loved) him she _____ (stayed/would have stayed) with Bob.
